data.body: Implemented src/rebar/_snapshot/cache.py (read/populate path only; eviction is sibling S2b). acquire(ref, source_mode, repo_root, fetch) is the cached entry point gates call instead of S1.materialize. AC mapping: AC1 in-process per-SHA threading.Lock single-flight (pinned in isolation by test_in_process_lock_collapses_without_flock with flock disabled); AC2 cross-process flock(LOCK_EX) on locks/<sha>.lock (proven by 3 real subprocesses racing -> exactly one build); AC3 POSIX delete-on-last-close — open_in_snapshot opens fd up front, eviction is rename-away+rmtree, open fd survives; AC4 ENOENT/read-error -> CacheMiss -> miss self-heals via re-materialize; AC5 touch-on-read mtime via os.utime (never atime), no PID/heartbeat lease (asserted no getpid/kill call); AC6 byte-total incremented exactly once per real populate under flock read-modify-write+os.replace (atomicity proven under concurrency). Double-checked locking re-verifies dest after each lock. Subagent review: NO blocking issues, all 6 ACs verified met+correct; added 2 tests per its non-blocking notes (in-process-lock isolation, concurrent add_bytes atomicity). 12 unit tests pass; ruff clean.
